steps.description: Type 'tab app with azure backend' into the Samples search/filter input box in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit pane of Visual Studio Code.

Click the "Create" button next to "Tab App with Azure Back..." under the Samples section in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension panel in Visual Studio Code.
And input ${{var:app_name:hello-world-tab-with-backend}} into the run time variable.

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: bash

```bash
FILE="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/hello-world-tab-with-backend/aad.manifest.json"
LINE_NUM=67
OLD_CONTENT="503"
NEW_CONTENT="504"

if [ ! -f "$FILE" ]; then
  echo "File not found: $FILE" >&2
  exit 1
fi

tmpfile=$(mktemp)
awk -v n=$LINE_NUM -v old="$OLD_CONTENT" -v new="$NEW_CONTENT" '{
    if (NR == n) {
        gsub(old, new)
    }
    print
}' "$FILE" > "$tmpfile" && mv "$tmpfile" "$FILE"

exit 0
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0

✓ Script executed successfully

steps.description: @code in file "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/aad.manifest.json", replace part of content "503" to "504" in line 67.

Trigger the "Command Palette" in Visual Studio Code by pressing the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+P in the context of the "hello-world-tab-with-backend" workspace, with the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit panel open on the left sidebar.
Type text 'debug: select and start' into the VS Code Command Palette input field to search for the "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" command.
Press the Enter key to select the highlighted "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Type the text "debug in teams (Chrome)" into the Visual Studio Code command palette input box at the top center of the interface.
Press the Enter key to select "Debug in Teams (Chrome)" from the debug configuration dropdown in Visual Studio Code’s command palette.
